generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance ManufacturingControlLoop : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Enables bidirectional interaction between Digital Twin and physical manufacturing system for autonomous adjustments via control algorithms implemented in C++/Java"
]

instance SimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Executes simulation models (e.g., MathWorks Simulink) for behavioral prediction and optimization of manufacturing processes"
]

instance StateMachineExecutor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Implements state-based logic (SysML v1.7/MontiArc Automaton) for dynamic system behavior modeling and decision-making"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance ProductionLineSensorArray : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects contextual data traces from manufacturing equipment including operational logs and sensor feeds"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ManufacturingLineDataTransmitted
]

instance IoTDeviceNetwork : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Aggregates real-time sensor data from IoT-enabled manufacturing components"
    DTDFVocab:producedData IoTDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance ManufacturingLineDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ProductionLineSensorArray
]

instance IoTDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IoTDeviceNetwork
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLFunctionalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Defines functional architecture of manufacturing system using SysML methodology across requirements, functional, and logical layers"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolchainEnabler
]

instance MontiArcComponentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Describes system components, interfaces, and composition using MontiArc ADL with behavioral extensions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcToolchainEnabler
]

instance SimulinkBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Implements detailed behavioral simulation for dynamic system analysis and control logic using MathWorks Simulink"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngineEnabler
]

instance ManufacturingContextualData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated and abstracted contextual data traces from physical manufacturing system for analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ManufacturingLineDataTransmitted, IoTDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance ManufacturingOperationalLogs : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured operational logs from manufacturing equipment for performance tracking and anomaly detection"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ManufacturingLineDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAnalysisEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLToolchainEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Integrates SysML tools for multi-layered system modeling across requirements, functional, and logical architectures"
    DTDFVocab:enables ManufacturingSystemDesignService, StakeholderVisualizationService
]

instance MontiArcToolchainEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Provides MontiArc ADL environment for component-based system architecture with behavioral modeling extensions"
    DTDFVocab:enables SystemArchitectureService, ComponentInteractionService
]

instance SimulationEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Executes simulation models (Simulink) for behavioral prediction and optimization of manufacturing processes"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionAndOptimizationService, ControlLogicService
]

instance DataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processes raw manufacturing data through aggregation, abstraction, and contextualization pipelines"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataInterpretationService, RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance DataAnalysisEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Analyzes operational logs and performance data for anomaly detection and trend analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService, PerformanceTrackingService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataInterpretationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Actively interprets and processes incoming manufacturing data from Digital Shadow for decision-making"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataContextualizationInsight, AutomatedDataAggregationAction
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real-time monitoring of manufacturing system state using processed data from Digital Shadow"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateInsight, PerformanceAlertAction
]

instance PredictionAndOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Uses simulation tools to predict system behavior and optimize operational parameters"
    DTDFVocab:provides BehaviorPredictionInsight, OptimizationRecommendationAction
]

instance ControlLogicService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides feedback mechanisms to physical manufacturing system for autonomous adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlFeedbackAction
]

instance StakeholderVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offers multi-perspective visualization of manufacturing system states and data trends"
    DTDFVocab:provides EngineerViewInsight, ManagerDashboardInsight
]

instance ManufacturingSystemDesignService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Supports system design, validation, and optimization using MBSE methodologies"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemDesignInsight
]

instance SystemArchitectureService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Manages component interactions and system composition using MontiArc ADL"
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentInteractionInsight
]

instance ComponentInteractionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Coordinates interactions between heterogeneous system components"
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentCoordinationInsight
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Detects anomalies in manufacturing operations through data analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyAlertInsight
]

instance PerformanceTrackingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Tracks system performance metrics and operational efficiency"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceMetricsInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance DataContextualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Provides contextualized manufacturing data for informed decision-making"
]

instance SystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time representation of manufacturing system operational state"
]

instance BehaviorPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted behavior of manufacturing system under various conditions"
]

    base:desc "Recommends operational parameter adjustments for improved efficiency"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

    base:desc "Autonomous control adjustments to manufacturing system based on DT analysis"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EngineerViewInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Technical visualization of manufacturing system for engineering analysis"
]

instance ManagerDashboardInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "High-level operational overview for management decision-making"
]

instance SystemDesignInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Multi-layered system design representation using MBSE methodologies"
]

instance ComponentInteractionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualization of component interactions and system composition"
]

instance ComponentCoordinationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Coordination mechanisms between heterogeneous system components"
]

instance AnomalyAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Alerts regarding detected anomalies in manufacturing operations"
]

instance PerformanceMetricsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Tracked performance metrics and operational efficiency indicators"
]

instance AutomatedDataAggregationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated aggregation of raw manufacturing data into actionable insights"

]

    base:desc "Automated alerts for performance deviations from expected thresholds"

]

// C1: Environment
instance ManufacturingDigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital Twin environment for manufacturing system encompassing all modeling, simulation, and control components"
    base:contains ManufacturingControlLoop, SimulationEngine, StateMachineExecutor,
                ProductionLineSensorArray, IoTDeviceNetwork,
                SysMLFunctionalArchitectureModel, MontiArcComponentModel, SimulinkBehavioralModel,
                ManufacturingContextualData, ManufacturingOperationalLogs
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin (DT) constellation** in this context is implicitly defined by the **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) framework**, which emphasizes **orchestration through multi-layered abstraction** (requirements, functional, logical, and product/technical layers). The architecture relies on **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML IBDs to define **component & connector perspectives**, including interfaces, interconnections, and composition. Behavioral coordination is achieved via **state machines (SysML v1.7), MontiArc Automaton, or external simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink). The system-wide coordination is **heterogeneous**, integrating **multiple tools and stakeholders**, and may require **active components** (as opposed to passive digital shadows) to process and interpret data flows. No explicit orchestration pattern (e.g., microservices, service-oriented architecture) is specified, but the modularity of MBSE suggests a **loosely coupled, service-oriented constellation** with **resource management** handled via tool-specific mechanisms (e.g., Simulink’s simulation scheduling or ADL-defined interfaces)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates **fidelity and validity considerations** through structured **validation and verification (V&V) methods** aligned with MBSE practices. Key aspects include:
  - **Model fidelity requirements**: Ensured via **multi-layered abstraction** (requirements → functional/logical → technical) and **tool-specific validation** (e.g., SysML state machines, Simulink simulations).
  - **Uncertainty quantification**: Addressed through **data aggregation and abstraction** from Digital Shadow traces, where automated one-way data flows (e.g., sensor/physical system → digital model) are passively collected and processed by active DT components for interpretation.
  - **Error detection/correction**: Implicitly supported by **heterogeneous tool integration** (e.g., ADLs for structural consistency, simulation tools for behavioral accuracy) and **stakeholder-specific validation** to reconcile discrepancies across modeling perspectives.
  - **Quality assurance processes**: Enforced via **traceability mechanisms** across modeling notations (e.g., SysML → Simulink) and **methodologies like RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)** to ensure alignment between physical and digital representations. No explicit mention of **quantitative uncertainty metrics** or **automated error correction protocols** is provided in the document."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for a Digital Twin (DT) system is not explicitly detailed in the provided documents. However, the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** [32] implies that the system must support **one-way data transmission** from physical to digital components, which may require underlying **communication protocols** (e.g., MQTT, OPC UA, or HTTP/REST) for IoT or industrial applications. The document does not specify **network topology/architecture**, **bandwidth/latency requirements**, or **reliability/redundancy mechanisms**, though the need for **active software components** [33], [34] suggests robustness in data handling. **Security protocols** (e.g., encryption, authentication) are not mentioned, but the integration of heterogeneous stakeholders [31] implies a need for secure data exchange."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **hosting and deployment infrastructure** for a Digital Twin (DT) system is not explicitly described in the provided documents. However, the **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** context [17]–[30] suggests that the system may leverage **cloud, edge, or on-premise platforms** depending on computational demands. While **scalability mechanisms** are implied (e.g., handling heterogeneous models and tools), no specific deployment models (e.g., containerization, microservices) or **availability/reliability assurances** (e.g., fault tolerance, high availability) are detailed. The document does not specify **computational/storage requirements**, though the integration of simulation tools (e.g., MathWorks Simulink [28]) and general-purpose languages (C++, Java) indicates resource-intensive operations."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The document highlights **horizontal integration** through the use of **heterogeneous modeling tools and languages** (e.g., SysML, MontiArc, Simulink, C++, Java) to describe different layers of system abstraction. **Interoperability** is implied via **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like AADL or MontiArc, which standardize component interfaces and connections. However, no **specific data exchange protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, REST) or **enterprise integration standards** (e.g., OAGIS, RosettaNet) are mentioned. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) focuses on **one-way data flows** from physical to digital objects, suggesting that **external system dependencies** are passive (e.g., sensors or legacy systems feeding data). Integration with other Digital Twins or enterprise systems is not explicitly detailed, but the MBSE approach implies **modular, tool-agnostic integration** via shared modeling notations (e.g., SysML for functional/logical layers). No explicit mention of **APIs, middleware, or real-time synchronization** is provided."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot incorporates **safety-critical system considerations** through the **safety cell**, which restricts the maximum working range of the first vertical axis to **[-90°, 90°]** to prevent collisions or unsafe operations. **Threat mitigation strategies** are implicitly addressed via the **dual-encoder sensor arrangement** (motor encoder and secondary encoder behind the gearbox), which reduces **backlash and positional uncertainty** by cross-referencing encoder data to infer **elasticity and friction** in the drive train, thereby improving system reliability. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are not explicitly detailed, but the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller**—used for axis control—likely includes built-in safety protocols for subtractive manufacturing (e.g., emergency stops, torque/position monitoring). **Risk assessment approaches** are not explicitly described, though the **gear transmission and cycloidal gear system** (with torque outputs **τ_G** and **τ_out**) suggests a focus on mechanical integrity and torque control to mitigate operational risks. **Cybersecurity protocols** and **access control mechanisms** are not mentioned, nor are **authentication systems** or **data protection regulations** (e.g., GDPR, ISO 27001)."
]
